cot_prefix: Okay, let's go through this step by step. The question is about fitting a working telephone booth. The options are A, B, C, D. First, the story mentions that the man from the telephone department got off the bus and went to the tea stall. Then, it says: "It was a year ago that the phone line had been installed, six months later men from the public works department had come to put up the phone booth." So the phone line was installed a year ago, then six months later the booth was put up. That would make the total time since the line was installed as 1.5 years. But the question is about fitting a working telephone booth. The phone booth was installed six months after the line, but then it was painted, and there's a mention of a painter taking a day to paint the sign. However, the man says later that the minister will come next month to inaugurate it. So the booth was installed but not yet working because it needed inauguration. But the question is about the process of fitting the booth. The phone line was a year ago, the booth six months later. So the process of fitting the booth took six months after the line? Or the entire process from line to booth was 18 months? Wait, the question is about fitting a working telephone booth. The phone line was installed a year ago, and the booth was put up six months after that. But the booth wasn't working yet because the minister has to inaugurate it. But the actual installation of the booth (the structure) was done six months after the line. So
